name: PitchWall
description: Startup pitch and launch board where founders post their product alongside an elevator pitch and the community discovers, upvotes and gives feedback; audience is founders, indie hackers and early adopters.

auth.notes: Free email/password account to post a pitch and manage your listing. Social sign-in may be available; posts can be upvoted and commented on by the community.

best_practices: PitchWall is a pitch board, so nail the elevator pitch: state the problem, your solution and why now in a few tight sentences.
Open the pitch with a single sentence a non-expert investor could repeat; clarity of positioning drives upvotes here.
Make the tagline outcome-focused (what the user gains) rather than feature-focused.
Quantify traction or a sharp insight if you have it; this founder audience responds to evidence and momentum.
Pick the category that matches your market so the right founders and early adopters see the pitch.
Add a clean logo and a screenshot or demo link so people can quickly grasp the product.
Reply to feedback fast; engaged threads get pushed higher on the board.

questions.label: Tagline
questions.help: One outcome-focused line on what users gain.

questions.label: Elevator pitch
questions.help: Problem, solution and why now, in a few tight sentences an investor could repeat.

questions.label: Description
questions.help: Short summary of the product for the listing.

questions.label: Demo / pitch video URL
questions.help: Optional short demo or pitch video.
